title: Mumbai 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Mumbai 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Mumbai, India: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Elephanta Caves and Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya. Prices from $1838 per person (estimate).
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Mumbai, India — Tropical climate moderated by the Arabian Sea: warm and humid nearly year-round (highs 29-33°C), with a very heavy monsoon June-September that can cause local flooding, and a relatively mild, dry winter (Dec-Feb). November to February for cooler, drier weather (highs 29-32°C); the June-September monsoon brings dramatic but disruptive rain and is best avoided for sightseeing. This itinerary blends Elephanta Caves, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya, Gateway of India with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Elephanta Caves, Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya, Gateway of India
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Mumbai
itinerary.description: Land at Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj International Airport (BOM) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Elephanta Caves — Rock-cut Shiva temple caves on an island, UNESCO World Heritage Site, reached by ferry. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya & Global Vipassana Pagoda
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Vastu Sangrahalaya — Indo-Saracenic museum of art, archaeology and natural history. In the afternoon, continue to Global Vipassana Pagoda — Massive golden meditation hall dome overlooking the harbor. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Jehangir Art Gallery & Essel World
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Jehangir Art Gallery — Leading contemporary art gallery in Kala Ghoda. In the afternoon, continue to Essel World — Large amusement and water park. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Elephanta Caves
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Elephanta Caves, a popular side trip from Mumbai. Return to Mumbai in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Khotachiwadi heritage lane — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Nehru Science Centre & Bollywood studio tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Nehru Science Centre — Interactive science museum with a planetarium. In the afternoon, continue to Bollywood studio tour — Behind-the-scenes look at a working Film City studio. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Lonavala and Khandala hill stations
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Lonavala and Khandala hill stations, a popular side trip from Mumbai. Return to Mumbai in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Lonavala and Khandala hill stations

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Sanjay Gandhi National Park & Gateway of India
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Sanjay Gandhi National Park — Forested reserve with the Kanheri Caves and a mini lion safari. In the afternoon, continue to Gateway of India — Iconic waterfront arch monument facing the Arabian Sea. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Banganga Tank at sunset — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Alibaug beach town
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Alibaug beach town, a popular side trip from Mumbai. Return to Mumbai in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Alibaug beach town

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Marine Drive & Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CST)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Marine Drive — Sweeping bayfront promenade known as the "Queen's Necklace" at night. In the afternoon, continue to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj Terminus (CST) — UNESCO-listed Victorian Gothic railway station. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Mumbai: Haji Ali Dargah & Dhobi Ghat
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Haji Ali Dargah — Mosque and shrine on an islet, reached by a causeway. In the afternoon, continue to Dhobi Ghat — World's largest open-air laundromat. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Mahim Nature Park — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Banganga Tank or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to BOM for your departure flight.

travelTips: Avoid peak rush hour (8:30-10:30am and 6-8:30pm) on suburban trains if carrying luggage.
Book Elephanta Caves ferry tickets in the morning — the last return ferry leaves mid-afternoon.
Bandra and Lower Parel have Mumbai's best contemporary dining and nightlife scenes.
Monsoon season (Jun-Sep) can cause waterlogging and flight delays — check forecasts before island-hopping day trips.
The Gateway of India area is best visited early morning or evening to avoid both heat and crowds.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light, breathable clothing for year-round humidity
A compact umbrella or raincoat if visiting June-September
Comfortable shoes for uneven pavements and station crowds
A modest outfit for visiting Haji Ali Dargah or Siddhivinayak Temple
thingsToDo: Trekking in Sanjay Gandhi National Park
Scuba diving day trips near Malvan (weekend excursion)
thingsToAvoid: Unlicensed taxi touts outside the airport — use the prepaid counter or a ride-hailing app.
Swimming at Juhu or Chowpatty beaches — currents and pollution make them unsafe for swimming.
Walking on train tracks or overcrowded platforms — Mumbai's rail system has real safety risks for the inattentive.
Overpriced "Bollywood audition" scams targeting tourists in Bandra and Colaba.
Traveling during severe monsoon flooding alerts — the city can grind to a halt.
instagramSpots: Gateway of India with the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel
familyActivities: Sanjay Gandhi National Park mini lion safari
adventureActivities: Trekking in Sanjay Gandhi National Park
Scuba diving day trips near Malvan (weekend excursion)
Paragliding near Kamshet (day trip from Mumbai)
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel
Private yacht cruise around Mumbai Harbour
Fine dining omakase or tasting menu in Bandra-Kurla Complex
Private Bollywood studio and set-visit experience
honeymoonExperiences: Private dinner cruise on Mumbai Harbour
Couple's spa at a Bandra luxury hotel
Rooftop dinner with a Bandra-Worli Sea Link view
budgetExperiences: Vada pav and street food crawl in Mohammed Ali Road
Free evening at Marine Drive and Chowpatty Beach
Local train ride through the suburbs

festivalCalendar.name: Ganesh Chaturthi
festivalCalendar.timing: August-September (per lunar calendar), Mumbai's biggest festival

localEtiquette: Dress modestly at religious sites such as Haji Ali Dargah and Siddhivinayak Temple.
Queue discipline on Mumbai's suburban trains is important — stand aside to let passengers exit before boarding.
Tipping 10% at restaurants is appreciated though not always automatically added.
Avoid discussing sensitive topics like the 2008 attacks casually at memorial sites such as the Taj Mahal Palace Hotel.
Remove shoes before entering temples and some traditional homes.
localTransportGuide: The suburban local train network is the fastest way across the city but gets extremely crowded at rush hour; the Mumbai Metro and monorail cover newer corridors. Ola/Uber and black-and-yellow metered taxis are widely available for point-to-point travel, and a ferry connects the Gateway of India to Elephanta Caves.
bestTimeToVisit: November to February for cooler, drier weather (highs 29-32°C); the June-September monsoon brings dramatic but disruptive rain and is best avoided for sightseeing.
weather: Tropical climate moderated by the Arabian Sea: warm and humid nearly year-round (highs 29-33°C), with a very heavy monsoon June-September that can cause local flooding, and a relatively mild, dry winter (Dec-Feb).
visaInformation: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel. Indian citizens need no visa, and OCI/PIO cardholders enter visa-free with a linked foreign passport — common for the large NRI diaspora with family or business ties to Mumbai.
averageFlightTime: ~9.7h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Mumbai?
faqs.answer: Most foreign nationals require an Indian e-Visa applied for online before travel. Indian citizens need no visa, and OCI/PIO cardholders enter visa-free with a linked foreign passport — common for the large NRI diaspora with family or business ties to Mumbai.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Mumbai?
faqs.answer: November to February for cooler, drier weather (highs 29-32°C); the June-September monsoon brings dramatic but disruptive rain and is best avoided for sightseeing. Tropical climate moderated by the Arabian Sea: warm and humid nearly year-round (highs 29-33°C), with a very heavy monsoon June-September that can cause local flooding, and a relatively mild, dry winter (Dec-Feb).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Mumbai safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Mumbai is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Mumbai?
faqs.answer: The local currency is INR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Isabella Kowalski
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Essel World and Global Vipassana Pagoda. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Anna Garcia
reviews.title: Consultant made it effortless
reviews.body: Mumbai is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Essel World. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Elena Fernandez
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Jehangir Art Gallery and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Liam Khan
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Our trip to Mumbai was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Elephanta Caves was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Priya Smith
reviews.title: Beautiful destination, well organized
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Bollywood studio tour in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.
